title: lomatium howellii
intro: Lomatium howellii, also known as Howell's lomatium or Howell's biscuitroot, is a perennial plant species native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, making it effective against various health issues. The medicinal properties of Lomatium howellii are attributed to its volatile oils and other compounds, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of the common cold, flu, and other respiratory issues, as well as to boost the immune system. Lomatium howellii can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and it is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
